vibe: Legendary local institution - plastic chairs cash only
insider_tip: Dubai institution since 1978 - the queue outside at midnight is a Dubai rite of passage. Politicians and labourers eat side by side here
One of the oldest restaurants in Dubai - the whole lamb on a skewer served tableside is extraordinary value at under 200 AED for 4 people

best_dish: Fried hammour fish with biryani rice
insider_tip: No menu - the waiter tells you what fish is fresh today. Loved by every Dubai resident and almost unknown to tourists. Cash only

best_dish: Balaleet sweet vermicelli breakfast or Chicken Machboos
insider_tip: The best place in Dubai for a traditional Emirati breakfast - try balaleet which is sweet saffron vermicelli with egg. Hidden in the Al Fahidi heritage district

best_dish: Crispy falafel wrap with garlic sauce
insider_tip: Best falafel in Dubai at the most reasonable price - the garlic sauce is legendary among Dubai residents. Multiple locations across the city

best_dish: Khameer bread with date jam and cream
insider_tip: The only modern Emirati casual restaurant in Dubai - try khameer the traditional Emirati bread. Perfect introduction to local food without formal dining prices
The best brunch spot in Dubai that locals actually use - in the industrial Al Quoz area far from tourist zones. Friday mornings are electric

best_dish: Any pasta dish or the brunch
insider_tip: Directly overlooking the Dubai Fountain - book a window table and time arrival for the 6pm show. Same fountain view as much more expensive restaurants

best_dish: Acai bowl or the eggs royale
insider_tip: Best people-watching brunch in Jumeirah - the organic garden terrace feels like Paris in a villa. Very popular with Dubai's health-conscious crowd
Best Greek food in Dubai by far and completely unknown to tourists. Outdoor lakeside seating with the whole JLT lake as your view. Half the price of similar quality in Dubai Marina
The most dramatic interior design of any mid-range restaurant in Dubai - the architectural Arabic lattice work and lighting is extraordinary. Good for impressing guests
The lunch set menu at 195 AED is an extraordinary deal for Nobu quality - barely mentioned and dramatically cheaper than dinner with the same kitchen
Dining surrounded by 65000 sea creatures in a floor-to-ceiling aquarium - the most visually unique dining experience in the Middle East. Book 2 weeks ahead

best_dish: Spicy beef tenderloin or rock shrimp
insider_tip: The bar area accepts walk-ins even when dining is fully booked - order the full menu at the bar and save 20% versus the formal dining room

best_dish: Lobster thermidor or catch of day
insider_tip: The only restaurant in Dubai where you can dine on the water with an unobstructed Burj Al Arab view from both sides of the table. Worth every dirham for a special occasion

best_dish: Ottoman steak - the show is the point
insider_tip: The food is secondary to the experience of watching the service ritual - go once for the story. The burger at 180 AED is the best value item on an otherwise extreme menu
Michelin-starred chef in Dubai - the pasta tasting menu is world-class at a fraction of equivalent European prices. One of Dubai's best-kept fine dining secrets
The most authentic Emirati dining experience in Dubai - in a desert fort outside the city with camel rides falconry and traditional music. Requires driving outside Dubai but unforgettable
The best healthy food brand in Dubai - quality of ingredients is exceptionally high and the outdoor seating areas are consistently beautiful. Very popular with Dubai's fitness community

best_dish: Full English or the signature cake platters
insider_tip: The most consistent all-day dining option across Dubai - quality and menu identical across 8 locations. The cake display is genuinely extraordinary
The best value fine dining with a Burj Al Arab view - substantially cheaper than neighboring Madinat restaurants with equivalent or better food quality
The terrace has one of the best Burj Khalifa views of any restaurant in the city and the wood-fired pizzas are genuinely exceptional - hugely popular with Dubai residents
Hidden in the art district - the most surprising restaurant in Dubai. Seasonal menu changes weekly. The kind of restaurant that would have a Michelin star in London

opening_hours: Literally dining suspended 50m above Dubai on a platform crane - check the schedule as it moves locations. The most dramatic dining concept in the city

best_dish: Afternoon tea or the tasting menu
insider_tip: At 442m this is the world's highest restaurant - the afternoon tea at 295 AED is the best value way to experience it versus dinner prices
